summary: Tested the Companion module Rotary STEP Pan/Tilt rewrite that uses ptAbsolute VISCA packets with the user's field-calibrated numbers (pan center 19050 / 108.74 units per deg, tilt center 8000 / 86.66 units per deg). Both the primary integration test (26/26 assertions) and an independent 4-scenario re-verification harness pass with exit code 0. Syntax checks (node --check) pass for both src/actions.js and src/config.js. No FastAPI/React app is involved — this is a Node ESM module.
critical_code_review_comments: src/actions.js is 767 lines — approaching the 700-line threshold; consider splitting STEP actions, HOLD/drive tracking, and preset actions into separate modules for future maintainability (non-blocking).

absoluteUnits() rounds AFTER adding to center and BEFORE toInt16 masking — this is correct for the calibration but relies on Math.round; the tests confirm off-by-one only appears at ±175° extreme (panU=21 vs raw 20.5), which is within the user's ±5 tolerance for the LEFT extreme.
sendPtAbsoluteThrottled coalesces on a 30 ms window using a single timer; this is why 37 rapid clicks emit exactly one packet — verified in Test 2 and Scenario B.
toInt16 correctly wraps positive units > 32767 into negative int16 (verified at +175° producing panU = -27456), matching how the camera reports positions on the wire.

Config schema exposes panCenter, panUnitsPerDeg, panMin/MaxDeg, tiltCenter, tiltUnitsPerDeg, tiltMin/MaxDeg, panDegPerSec, tiltDegPerSec — all with sane defaults matching the user's NDI camera. Signs on units-per-degree can be flipped to invert direction (verified in Test 7).
updated_files: /app/companion-module-tenveo-ptz/test/independent-verify.test.js (new — independent re-verification of the four review-request claims using fresh module instances)
test_credentials: n/a — no auth involved; camera hardware is on the user's LAN and not reachable from this container, so all testing uses fake sockets in-process.
seed_data_creation: None. Tests instantiate a fake `self` object with the default calibration inline.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run `cd /app/companion-module-tenveo-ptz && node test/step-calibration.test.js` (primary, 26 assertions) and `node test/independent-verify.test.js` (4 scenarios, redundant sanity). Both must exit 0. There is no supervisor service to restart for this module; the /app/backend FastAPI stub is unrelated. If the user later adjusts calibration defaults, update the expected values in step-calibration.test.js accordingly.
rca_of_the_issue: Original bug: STEP Pan/Tilt used relative moves (ptRelative) whose per-step units did not correspond to real degrees on the VHD20HAN NDI-variant camera, so N clicks did not sum to N × deg-per-click. Fix (already in place): actions now maintain an integer state.panDeg / state.tiltDeg counter clamped to [-175..+175] and [-90..+90], compute VISCA units via panCenter + panDeg × panUnitsPerDeg (masked to signed 16-bit), and emit exactly one coalesced ptAbsolute packet per ~30 ms window carrying the latest target. Verified: single click right → 19159; 37 clicks right → one packet, panU=23073; 200 clicks left → clamped -175, panU≈21 (matches user's physical LEFT extreme ~20); 10 clicks tilt up → tiltU=8867; +175° wraps to int16 (-27456); flipping sign of units-per-degree inverts direction; pt_step_reset zeroes counters and sends no packet; VISCA nibble encoding of ptAbsolute is byte-exact.
